CONCLUSION: Among the measures computed on the synthetic data,
the lowest mean percentile rank for Nobel prize papers was achieved by 'Pi5' (42.86%).
This aligns with the paper's finding that centrality-based measures on larger ego networks
can better discern award-winning work than the CD Index alone.
